Quantum Space
Leadership Hire
Quantum Space agrees on transaction that will take it public
xAI
Growth Prediction
It is probable that xAI will grow
Perplexity
Growth Insight
Perplexity reported that its revenue grew from $100 million to $500 million, indicating a fivefold increase tied to recent AI-native growth, while headcount rose only 34%. The AI answer engine generated about $200 million in annualized revenue from its subscription-focused business model. Perplexity's annual recurring revenue grew 4.7 times year-over-year, reaching approximately $200 million by late 2025, reflecting rapid monetization of its AI chatbot via subscriptions and enterprise customers. The company expanded its customer base to over 100 million monthly active users, indicating rising adoption of its AI search and agent products. Perplexity's user traffic surged from 52.4 million to 159.7 million monthly visits between March 2024 and March 2025, reflecting rapid global adoption of its AI search service.

Nala
Growth Insight
NALA raised a total of $40 million in a Series A funding round to support its cross-border payments and infrastructure expansion. Additionally, NALA secured a scalable credit facility of up to $50 million, with an initial $25 million tranche, from Liquidity via Mars Growth Capital to pre-fund transfers and expand its stablecoin-based payment network. The company expanded its market presence in Nigeria by securing an International Money Transfer Operator license from the Central Bank of Nigeria and integrating with NIBSS, enabling direct real-time remittances into local bank accounts and wallets nationwide. Furthermore, NALA expanded its operations into the Ghanaian market after receiving regulatory approval from the Bank of Ghana and partnering with BigPay to process cross-border payments.

Serve Robotics
Growth Insight
Serve Robotics expanded its operations significantly by acquiring Diligent Robotics, a healthcare robotics company, in a deal valued at approximately $29 million. This acquisition aimed to broaden its multi-vertical robotics platform and enhance recurring revenue streams. The company completed over 100,000 autonomous deliveries from more than 2,500 restaurants across several U.S. cities, indicating a substantial customer adoption base for its sidewalk delivery robots. Serve Robotics reported a fourth-quarter adjusted loss of $0.34 per share, which was narrower than the expected loss of $0.53 per share. Additionally, the company generated $2.7 million in revenue in 2025 and projected a rapid ramp to about $26 million in 2026 as it scaled its delivery robot deployments. Serve Robotics also expanded its geographic footprint by launching operations in Chicago and South Florida, and it entered Alexandria, Virginia, extending its coverage in the Washington, D.C. area.

Temasek Holdings

Meta
Growth Insight
Meta Platforms reported a first-quarter revenue of $56.31 billion for 2026, reflecting a 33% increase year over year from approximately $42.34 billion. This growth was driven by a 19% rise in ad impressions and a 12% increase in average ad prices. The company also generated a net income of $26.8 billion, marking a 61% increase compared to the previous year. Despite these positive financial results, Meta faced challenges, including a decline in daily active users across its family of apps, which fell to 3.56 billion from 3.58 billion in the prior quarter, marking the first decline since 2019. Additionally, Meta announced plans to lay off about 8,000 employees, roughly 10% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring to focus on AI investments and efficiency improvements. The company also completed a $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus, although this deal was later ordered to be unwound by Chinese regulators due to national security concerns.

Cyera

OpenEvidence
Growth Insight
OpenEvidence reported significant growth in its user base and funding. The company raised $250 million in a Series D funding round co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, doubling its valuation to $12 billion. OpenEvidence's AI medical search tool experienced a surge in monthly clinical consultation usage, increasing from 3 million to 18 million within a year. The platform is now utilized by approximately 650,000 doctors in the US and 1.2 million doctors internationally, marking a substantial expansion of its physician user base. Additionally, the AI-powered medical search platform supported over 1 million clinical consultations in a single day, indicating large-scale usage among verified physicians.

Suno
IPO Prediction

Bolt
Growth Insight
Bolt grew its South African user base to 30 million passengers and onboarded 500,000 drivers over ten years, reflecting strong adoption of its ride-hailing platform. In its first year of operations in Malaysia, Bolt reported a 40% quarter-on-quarter growth in completed rides and a 25% increase in active driver partners. Additionally, Bolt saw airport ride volumes grow during Nigeria’s Detty December period, with Lagos trips up 14.9% and Abuja up 17.56% between November 20 and December 5, 2025. The company also reported an 8% surge in ride demand in December 2025 in Kenya. Bolt’s Budapest fleet expanded from about 900 cars in 2019 to more than 3,000 by 2024, indicating a substantial increase in active drivers. Furthermore, Bolt expanded its food delivery service in Bulgaria, reaching over 1,000 partner restaurants nationwide since launch, with more than 150 available to Varna users.

It is very likely that General Atlantic will grow
Neuralink
Growth Insight
Neuralink expanded its human clinical trial participation from 12 users in September to 21 participants worldwide, indicating growing adoption of its implant among patients. The company raised a total of $650 million in a Series E funding round in June 2025, which implied a company valuation of $9 billion. This funding was aimed at supporting broader clinical testing and preparation for the commercial rollout of its brain-computer interface implants. Additionally, Neuralink achieved a key R&D milestone by successfully enabling its first human patient to control a computer cursor and a robotic arm using only their thoughts, marking significant progress in its neurotechnology program.

Honor
Growth Insight
Honor achieved significant growth in smartphone shipments, reaching 71 million units sold in 2025, which represented a 50% year-over-year increase. The company also reported a 24% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in Q1 2026, making it the fastest-growing manufacturer among the global top ten. Honor's overseas smartphone sales rose by 55% year-on-year in the first three quarters of 2025, with notable growth in the Middle East, Africa, and Southeast Asia. Additionally, Honor's smartphone market share increased to over 18% in Malaysia, where it became the market leader, and it surpassed 10% market share in 17 countries across Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa. The company also recorded a 73% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in the Middle East in Q1 2026, boosting its position to second place in the region's handset market.

Parabilis Medicines
Growth Insight
Parabilis Medicines raised $305 million in an oversubscribed Series F financing round to advance its cancer peptide therapy zolucatetide and support its discovery pipeline. The financing round was co-led by RA Capital Management, Fidelity Management & Research Company, and Janus Henderson Investors. This funding was part of a total of $780 million disclosed by local biotechs ahead of the JPMorgan conference. Parabilis Medicines has raised over $800 million in prior private financings from various investors. Additionally, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals committed $125 million to Parabilis Medicines, which included a $50 million upfront payment and a $75 million equity financing commitment as part of a broader strategic collaboration to develop antibody-helicon conjugate therapies. The company also initiated its IPO process, targeting gross proceeds of about $413–$476 million.

Horizon Robotics
Growth Insight
Horizon Robotics accumulated over 25,000 activations of its HSD assisted-driving solution within 8 weeks of its mass-market launch, with 77% of buyers of HSD-optional models choosing the HSD-equipped version. The company completed its IPO in 2024 at a valuation of approximately $6.7 billion, and its market capitalization grew to around $13 billion by April 2026. In 2025, Horizon Robotics reported a revenue increase of 57.7% year over year, reaching 3.758 billion CNY, driven by a surge in automotive AI chip shipments that exceeded 4 million units. The company maintained a 47.7% share of China’s self-owned-brand ADAS market, retaining its top position and ranking among the top three suppliers of high-level urban NOA intelligent-driving chips.

Growth Insight
Sonar acquired AI-native code review platform Gitar to integrate its capabilities into Sonar’s AI code verification and governance offerings while keeping Gitar as a standalone product. This acquisition aimed to enhance Sonar's technical debt management offering by adding AI-native code review capabilities that automatically flag issues, generate fixes, validate them in CI, and commit to branches. Additionally, Sonar grew its revenues from €125.834 million in 2021 to €288.992 million in 2024.

Growth Insight
Eisen raised a total of $18.5 million in funding, which included a $10 million Series A round led by MissionOG and an earlier $8.5 million seed round led by Index Ventures. The funding was aimed at expanding its AI-enabled compliance infrastructure platform and operations for financial institutions. The company also secured $7.2 million in a separate funding round disclosed in an SEC filing, which was part of a planned $10.5 million offering involving six investors.

Honor
Growth Insight
Honor achieved significant growth in smartphone shipments, reaching 71 million units sold in 2025, which represented a 50% year-over-year increase. The company also reported a 24% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in Q1 2026, making it the fastest-growing manufacturer among the global top ten. Honor's overseas smartphone sales rose by 55% year-on-year in the first three quarters of 2025, with notable growth in the Middle East, Africa, and Southeast Asia. Additionally, Honor's smartphone market share increased to over 18% in Malaysia, where it became the market leader, and it surpassed 10% market share in 17 countries across Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa. The company also recorded a 73% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in the Middle East in Q1 2026, boosting its position to second place in the region's handset market.

Parabilis Medicines
Growth Insight
Parabilis Medicines raised $305 million in an oversubscribed Series F financing round to advance its cancer peptide therapy zolucatetide and support its discovery pipeline. The financing round was co-led by RA Capital Management, Fidelity Management & Research Company, and Janus Henderson Investors. This funding was part of a total of $780 million disclosed by local biotechs ahead of the JPMorgan conference. Parabilis Medicines has raised over $800 million in prior private financings from various investors. Additionally, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals committed $125 million to Parabilis Medicines, which included a $50 million upfront payment and a $75 million equity financing commitment as part of a broader strategic collaboration to develop antibody-helicon conjugate therapies. The company also initiated its IPO process, targeting gross proceeds of about $413–$476 million.

Growth Insight
Horizon Robotics accumulated over 25,000 activations of its HSD assisted-driving solution within 8 weeks of its mass-market launch, with 77% of buyers of HSD-optional models choosing the HSD-equipped version. The company completed its IPO in 2024 at a valuation of approximately $6.7 billion, and its market capitalization grew to around $13 billion by April 2026. In 2025, Horizon Robotics reported a revenue increase of 57.7% year over year, reaching 3.758 billion CNY, driven by a surge in automotive AI chip shipments that exceeded 4 million units. The company maintained a 47.7% share of China’s self-owned-brand ADAS market, retaining its top position and ranking among the top three suppliers of high-level urban NOA intelligent-driving chips.

Perplexity
Growth Insight
Perplexity reported that its revenue grew from $100 million to $500 million, indicating a fivefold increase tied to recent AI-native growth, while headcount rose only 34%. The AI answer engine generated about $200 million in annualized revenue from its subscription-focused business model. Perplexity's annual recurring revenue grew 4.7 times year-over-year, reaching approximately $200 million by late 2025, reflecting rapid monetization of its AI chatbot via subscriptions and enterprise customers. The company expanded its customer base to over 100 million monthly active users, indicating rising adoption of its AI search and agent products. Perplexity's user traffic surged from 52.4 million to 159.7 million monthly visits between March 2024 and March 2025, reflecting rapid global adoption of its AI search service.

Growth Insight
NALA raised a total of $40 million in a Series A funding round to support its cross-border payments and infrastructure expansion. Additionally, NALA secured a scalable credit facility of up to $50 million, with an initial $25 million tranche, from Liquidity via Mars Growth Capital to pre-fund transfers and expand its stablecoin-based payment network. The company expanded its market presence in Nigeria by securing an International Money Transfer Operator license from the Central Bank of Nigeria and integrating with NIBSS, enabling direct real-time remittances into local bank accounts and wallets nationwide. Furthermore, NALA expanded its operations into the Ghanaian market after receiving regulatory approval from the Bank of Ghana and partnering with BigPay to process cross-border payments.

Serve Robotics
Growth Insight
Serve Robotics expanded its operations significantly by acquiring Diligent Robotics, a healthcare robotics company, in a deal valued at approximately $29 million. This acquisition aimed to broaden its multi-vertical robotics platform and enhance recurring revenue streams. The company completed over 100,000 autonomous deliveries from more than 2,500 restaurants across several U.S. cities, indicating a substantial customer adoption base for its sidewalk delivery robots. Serve Robotics reported a fourth-quarter adjusted loss of $0.34 per share, which was narrower than the expected loss of $0.53 per share. Additionally, the company generated $2.7 million in revenue in 2025 and projected a rapid ramp to about $26 million in 2026 as it scaled its delivery robot deployments. Serve Robotics also expanded its geographic footprint by launching operations in Chicago and South Florida, and it entered Alexandria, Virginia, extending its coverage in the Washington, D.C. area.

Meta
Growth Insight
Meta Platforms reported a first-quarter revenue of $56.31 billion for 2026, reflecting a 33% increase year over year from approximately $42.34 billion. This growth was driven by a 19% rise in ad impressions and a 12% increase in average ad prices. The company also generated a net income of $26.8 billion, marking a 61% increase compared to the previous year. Despite these positive financial results, Meta faced challenges, including a decline in daily active users across its family of apps, which fell to 3.56 billion from 3.58 billion in the prior quarter, marking the first decline since 2019. Additionally, Meta announced plans to lay off about 8,000 employees, roughly 10% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring to focus on AI investments and efficiency improvements. The company also completed a $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus, although this deal was later ordered to be unwound by Chinese regulators due to national security concerns.

Growth Insight
OpenEvidence reported significant growth in its user base and funding. The company raised $250 million in a Series D funding round co-led by Thrive Capital and DST Global, doubling its valuation to $12 billion. OpenEvidence's AI medical search tool experienced a surge in monthly clinical consultation usage, increasing from 3 million to 18 million within a year. The platform is now utilized by approximately 650,000 doctors in the US and 1.2 million doctors internationally, marking a substantial expansion of its physician user base. Additionally, the AI-powered medical search platform supported over 1 million clinical consultations in a single day, indicating large-scale usage among verified physicians.

Bolt
Growth Insight
Bolt grew its South African user base to 30 million passengers and onboarded 500,000 drivers over ten years, reflecting strong adoption of its ride-hailing platform. In its first year of operations in Malaysia, Bolt reported a 40% quarter-on-quarter growth in completed rides and a 25% increase in active driver partners. Additionally, Bolt saw airport ride volumes grow during Nigeria’s Detty December period, with Lagos trips up 14.9% and Abuja up 17.56% between November 20 and December 5, 2025. The company also reported an 8% surge in ride demand in December 2025 in Kenya. Bolt’s Budapest fleet expanded from about 900 cars in 2019 to more than 3,000 by 2024, indicating a substantial increase in active drivers. Furthermore, Bolt expanded its food delivery service in Bulgaria, reaching over 1,000 partner restaurants nationwide since launch, with more than 150 available to Varna users.

This funding was aimed at supporting broader clinical testing and preparation for the commercial rollout of its brain-computer interface implants. Additionally, Neuralink achieved a key R&D milestone by successfully enabling its first human patient to control a computer cursor and a robotic arm using only their thoughts, marking significant progress in its neurotechnology program.

Meta
Growth Insight
Meta Platforms reported a first-quarter revenue of $56.31 billion for 2026, reflecting a 33% increase year over year from approximately $42.34 billion. This growth was driven by a 19% rise in ad impressions and a 12% increase in average ad prices. The company also generated a net income of $26.8 billion, marking a 61% increase compared to the previous year. Despite these positive financial results, Meta faced challenges, including a decline in daily active users across its family of apps, which fell to 3.56 billion from 3.58 billion in the prior quarter, marking the first decline since 2019. Additionally, Meta announced plans to lay off about 8,000 employees, roughly 10% of its workforce, as part of a restructuring to focus on AI investments and efficiency improvements. The company also completed a $2 billion acquisition of AI startup Manus, although this deal was later ordered to be unwound by Chinese regulators due to national security concerns.

Bolt
Growth Insight
Bolt grew its South African user base to 30 million passengers and onboarded 500,000 drivers over ten years, reflecting strong adoption of its ride-hailing platform. In its first year of operations in Malaysia, Bolt reported a 40% quarter-on-quarter growth in completed rides and a 25% increase in active driver partners. Additionally, Bolt saw airport ride volumes grow during Nigeria’s Detty December period, with Lagos trips up 14.9% and Abuja up 17.56% between November 20 and December 5, 2025. The company also reported an 8% surge in ride demand in December 2025 in Kenya. Bolt’s Budapest fleet expanded from about 900 cars in 2019 to more than 3,000 by 2024, indicating a substantial increase in active drivers. Furthermore, Bolt expanded its food delivery service in Bulgaria, reaching over 1,000 partner restaurants nationwide since launch, with more than 150 available to Varna users.

Honor
Growth Insight
Honor achieved significant growth in smartphone shipments, reaching 71 million units sold in 2025, which represented a 50% year-over-year increase. The company also reported a 24% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in Q1 2026, making it the fastest-growing manufacturer among the global top ten. Honor's overseas smartphone sales rose by 55% year-on-year in the first three quarters of 2025, with notable growth in the Middle East, Africa, and Southeast Asia. Additionally, Honor's smartphone market share increased to over 18% in Malaysia, where it became the market leader, and it surpassed 10% market share in 17 countries across Latin America, the Middle East, and Africa. The company also recorded a 73% year-on-year growth in smartphone shipments in the Middle East in Q1 2026, boosting its position to second place in the region's handset market.

Parabilis Medicines
Growth Insight
Parabilis Medicines raised $305 million in an oversubscribed Series F financing round to advance its cancer peptide therapy zolucatetide and support its discovery pipeline. The financing round was co-led by RA Capital Management, Fidelity Management & Research Company, and Janus Henderson Investors. This funding was part of a total of $780 million disclosed by local biotechs ahead of the JPMorgan conference. Parabilis Medicines has raised over $800 million in prior private financings from various investors. Additionally, Regeneron Pharmaceuticals committed $125 million to Parabilis Medicines, which included a $50 million upfront payment and a $75 million equity financing commitment as part of a broader strategic collaboration to develop antibody-helicon conjugate therapies. The company also initiated its IPO process, targeting gross proceeds of about $413–$476 million.

Horizon Robotics
Growth Insight
Horizon Robotics accumulated over 25,000 activations of its HSD assisted-driving solution within 8 weeks of its mass-market launch, with 77% of buyers of HSD-optional models choosing the HSD-equipped version. The company completed its IPO in 2024 at a valuation of approximately $6.7 billion, and its market capitalization grew to around $13 billion by April 2026. In 2025, Horizon Robotics reported a revenue increase of 57.7% year over year, reaching 3.758 billion CNY, driven by a surge in automotive AI chip shipments that exceeded 4 million units. The company maintained a 47.7% share of China’s self-owned-brand ADAS market, retaining its top position and ranking among the top three suppliers of high-level urban NOA intelligent-driving chips.
